rationale: A web service account (svc-web, uid 33) opened an su session to user-01 (EV-001), after which user-01 successfully executed 'sudo cat /etc/shadow' as root (A0, EV-002 confirmed via DER-001, EV-003). The working directory (/var/www/intranet.corp.local/wp-content/uploads/2022/02) strongly suggests the initial foothold was a web shell or file upload exploit on a WordPress instance, with the attacker then escalating privileges to read the system password hash file. The full chain — web process → lateral user switch → sudo privilege escalation → credential harvesting — is confirmed by audit logs showing status 'success' (EV-002) and a root session opened (EV-003), leaving no benign explanation for this sequence.
